Maternal Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH)
Maternal Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH)
Overview
MCAH provides Mariposa County women, children and families’ assistance in accessing care; providing education to insure healthy living; and referrals to county resources.
Services
Healthy Families of America (HFA)
is a free program for eligible pregnant woman & families with children up to 5 years old. A registered nurse will visit your home to provide information on many topics & answer your questions.
The California Home Visiting Program (CHVP)
supports high-risk families to prevent Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs). It provides trained professionals to help parents during pregnancy and early childhood, promoting positive parenting and child development. Research shows it improves long-term outcomes for children and families.
Costs
Services are provided free to all eligible families. Participation is voluntary.
Health Topics
Immunization
Oral Health
Tobacco Education Program
Communicable Diseases
More information
The benefit of healthy eating adding up over time. Start simply by checking out my plate and learn more about how you can make every bite count at the
MyPlate website.
The
CHA
is a comprehensive health assessment using public data, interviews, surveys, and input to identify community challenges. Its findings guide the
CHIP
, which outlines targeted plans to improve community health. CHIP priorities are chosen by a diverse group of local stakeholders.
